content:
Suzanne Vega has some intense lyrics:

Solitude stands in the doorway
And I'm struck once again by her black silhouette
By her long cool stare and her silence
I suddenly remember each time we've met
And she turns to me with her hand extended
Her palm is split with a flower with a flame

The hallucinatory last line flips it from being an allegory to something more like a dream. It's like "You think you know what I'm doing here? No..."

https://www.youtube.com/watch?reload=9&v=Vse7-fjlkDQ
